mnemonics: Noisy Neighbors Can't Learn Properly: Remember that noise (Noisy) in the data (Neighbors) confuses (Can't) the learning algorithm (Learn) and prevents it from finding the correct underlying pattern (Properly).
keyPoints: Noise is irrelevant, erroneous, or random data that obscures the true underlying pattern.
Overfitting to noise leads to poor generalization on new, clean data.
Techniques like regularization, pruning, and early stopping help models become robust to noise.
commonMistakes: Treating noise as a pattern and overfitting the model to it.
Applying noise-handling techniques without first trying to clean the data at the source.
